---
title: "apply_mask"
generated: true
description: "Mask a tensor along an axis."
---

[‹ back to `nitrix.numerics.tensor_ops`](../nitrix-numerics-tensor_ops.qmd)

*Re-exported here; defined in `nitrix._internal.util`.*

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:apply_mask -->
```python
apply_mask(tensor: Shaped[Array, ...], msk: Bool[Array, ...], axis: int) -> Shaped[Array, ...]
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:apply_mask -->
Mask a tensor along an axis.

::: {.callout-warning}
This function will only work if the mask is one-dimensional. For
multi-dimensional masks, use [`conform_mask`](../nitrix-numerics-tensor_ops/conform_mask.qmd).
::
::: {.callout-warning}
Use of this function is strongly discouraged. It is incompatible with
`jax.jit`.
::
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:apply_mask -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|------------|--------|--------|----------|--------------------------------------------------------------|
| `tensor` | `Shaped[Array, ...]` | — | — | — |
| `msk` | `Bool[Array, ...]` | — | — | — |
| `axis` | `int` | — | — | — |

**Returns** `Shaped[Array, ...]`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen sections:apply_mask -->
### See also

[`conform_mask`](../nitrix-numerics-tensor_ops/conform_mask.qmd)
[`mask_tensor`](../nitrix-numerics-tensor_ops/mask_tensor.qmd)
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->
